titles.title: Mass-manufacturing and quality assurance solutions in SOFC value chain in FCH JU projects qSOFC and INNO-SOFC

descriptions.description: This poster presents mass-manufacturing and quality assurance solutions developed across the SOFC (Solid Oxide Fuel Cell) value chain within two FCH JU (Fuel Cells and Hydrogen Joint Undertaking) projects: qSOFC and INNO-SOFC. The poster covers advances across four areas: (1) Cells — automated machine vision for defect detection, targeting <400 €/kW cost and >95% yield; (2) Interconnects — FMEA, automated laser-scanning, and leak-tightness testing; (3) Stacks — optimization of assembly and conditioning processes; (4) Systems — Convion 60 kW system achieving >60% electrical efficiency and >85% total efficiency with 4000 €/kW target cost.

Automated machine vision inspection for cell defects. FMEA analysis, automated laser-scanning, and integrated leak-tightness measurement for interconnects. Testing and statistical analysis of three manufacturing/conditioning procedures using 30 stacks. Collaborative system design across the value chain.

fundingReferences.funderName: Fuel Cells and Hydrogen Joint Undertaking (FCH JU)

sections.sectionContent: Targets: Reduce cell manufacturing cost to 400 €/kW at 10 MW/year production; increase production yield >95% by automation and QA. Methods: Automated machine vision inspection for fault detection; optimization for high-speed manufacturing. Results: Higher and more homogeneous cell quality; higher yield and lower scrap rate. Partners: Elcogen, HaikuTech.

Targets: Interconnect manufacturing yield 95%; development and validation of QA procedures. Methods: FMEA; automated laser-scanning of geometries; integrated leak tightness measurement. Results: Improved quality; reduced scrap rate. Partners: MüKo Maschinenbau, ElringKlinger, SANDVIK.

Targets: Streamline stack manufacturing; cut cost; manufacture stacks for 60 kW INNO-SOFC system. Methods: Testing and analysis of three manufacturing/conditioning procedures using 30 stacks; statistical analysis. Results: Optimized stack assembly and conditioning processes identified. Partner: Elcogen.

Targets: Electrical efficiency 60%; total efficiency 85%; system cost 4000 €/kW. Methods: Effective value-chain collaboration; key components specifically designed and optimized. Results: System conceptual design ready, exceeds targets; significant reduction in parts count and assembly time. Partners: Convion, EnergyMatters.
